result.summary: boxyhq.com currently serves Ory/Polis developer docs and exposes documented HTTP API surfaces for Ory Polis REST and Directory Sync, both authenticated with self-configured API keys; no public GraphQL API, MCP server endpoint, or CLI specific to boxyhq.com was confirmed.
result.description: BoxyHQ has been acquired into Ory, and boxyhq.com now presents Ory documentation and product pages for identity and access management. Its Polis product handles enterprise SSO federation and SCIM-based directory sync for SaaS applications.

polis_api_key.label: Ory Polis API key
polis_api_key.setup: For self-hosted Ory Polis, define one or more API keys in the service configuration with `API_KEYS` (or deprecated `JACKSON_API_KEYS`). The docs say API requests to `/api/v1/` must send one of those keys as `Authorization: Api-Key <key>`. See [Environment Variables for Enterprise SSO](https://boxyhq.com/docs/polis/deploy/env-variables).

surfaces.name: Ory Polis REST API
surfaces.notes: Docs navigation exposes a REST API reference at `/docs/polis/reference/api`. The environment variable docs state API requests to `/api/v1/` are protected by API keys. The service quickstart indicates Polis can run as a separate service, but the public docs scraped here did not expose a machine-readable OpenAPI document or a canonical base URL beyond examples such as health checks on `http://localhost:{PORT}` and embedded examples.

surfaces.name: Ory Polis Directory Sync API
surfaces.notes: Docs navigation exposes a dedicated Directory Sync API reference. The Next.js guide shows directory creation and returns per-directory `scim.endpoint` and `scim.secret` values for customers to configure their IdP's SCIM client; those SCIM credentials are issued per directory and consumed by the IdP, not developer-minted integration credentials for calling Ory Polis' own developer API, so they are not recorded as separate credentials here. The scraped docs did not expose a machine-readable spec or a single canonical base URL.
